characters.name: open/close all folders
characters.description: The comedian of whom the series tells a fictionalized recollection of his childhood. He provides exposition, commentary, and the internal thoughts of his younger self and others. In "Everybody Hates The Guidance Counselor", he plays a guidance counselor assigned to Chris, basically serving to talk some sense into his younger self. First-Person Smartass: A good chunk of the series' snark comes from him. Large Ham: Some of his commentary has him raising his voice andChewing the Scenery, particularly when things get intense, hilarious,or both. Lemony Narrator: He doesn't just narrate the show; he snarks about his younger self's misfortune, dumb decisions, and the craziness of everyone and everything around him. Self-Deprecation: A lot of his commentary on himself, young and current, is this.

characters.name: The Narrator
characters.description: The comedian of whom the series tells a fictionalized recollection of his childhood. He provides exposition, commentary, and the internal thoughts of his younger self and others. In "Everybody Hates The Guidance Counselor", he plays a guidance counselor assigned to Chris, basically serving to talk some sense into his younger self. First-Person Smartass: A good chunk of the series' snark comes from him. Large Ham: Some of his commentary has him raising his voice andChewing the Scenery, particularly when things get intense, hilarious,or both. Lemony Narrator: He doesn't just narrate the show; he snarks about his younger self's misfortune, dumb decisions, and the craziness of everyone and everything around him. Self-Deprecation: A lot of his commentary on himself, young and current, is this.

characters.name: Chris
characters.description: The comedian Chris Rock is the main protagonist, appearing as a fictionalized version of himself. His adult self narrates the misadventures of his younger self, who is growing up in Bedford-Stuyvesant, Brooklyn during the 1980s. Life isn't easy between living with a quirky family in the ghetto, attending a mostly white school, and having a great deal of bad luck. Age Lift: Invoked by Chris Rock himself. Chris in real life was born on February 7, 1965, turning 13 in 1978. He states that if he set the show inThe '70sinstead, everyone would be "wearing afros and disco suits", so he settled for aging himself down to make the settingThe '80s. All for Nothing: Number of times, he makes big efforts for poor results. All of the Other Reindeer: He's bullied by both classmates and neighborhood thugs. Author Avatar: The series as a whole is asemi-autobiographical, semi-fictionalizedretelling of his adolescence.

characters.name: Julius
characters.description: Chris' father, and Rochelle's husband. He works as a newspaper delivery man and a security guard. Allergic to Routine: Most of why he works so much is to be a good provider; but when his union goes on strike (and he's assumedly recieving strike pay), he cannotstandsitting around doing nothing. Proving that part of his workaholic nature is the need to be in action. Bald of Authority: He's the most powerful authority figure in the house next to Rochelle and bald. Some flashbacks show him with hair though. Batter Up!: Seems to be his weapon of choice for defending his family, notably when he hears everyone scream in "Everybody Hates Keisha", when threatening Malvo for harassing Chris, and when Malvo and Peaches visit. Beware the Nice Ones: Julius is the easygoing parent, but the kids are still afraid of getting on his bad side.Only in two episodes did Julius give Chris a beating as punishment for his misdeeds. Once, when Chris blew Julius’ lottery money on an arcade game costing the family a fortune, and again when Chris gets in trouble for telling dirty jokes at school. On this occasion, however, Julius was going to spare Chris stating that there will be trouble “next time” it happens but Rochelle interjects with “as far as I’m concerned, this time is next time” resulting in Chris getting beat by both parents.Chris often exaggerates this to make it seem like he'll kill him. In one episode, Chris has anImagine Spotwhere he imagines Julius tossing him out a window for driving the car without permission.When Chris almost gets him shot via lying about a scissor killer, Julius stretches Chris's behind for almost getting him shot and lying to a cop.He's shown to have five belts for infractions in case his kids commit them. These infractions are lying, stealing, lying and stealing, getting people pregnant and doing drugs (the one for doing drugs has agun strapped to it).Even Tonya isn't safe from this. In 1 episode he snaps at her for messing up his show and in late season 4

characters.name: Rochelle
characters.description: Chris' mother, and Julius' wife. She's a very strict, bossy, and overbearing housewife. Abusive Parents: Rochelle is quick to anger when she finds out her children are up to no good and can even explode over the smallest things. Shemocks them like a child when she's pissed off, deploys physical punishments to get them back in line using creative threats, and is so strict that her children are afraid to tell the truth when she's around, unknowningly digging them further into a hole. Since the show is a comedy, it's hard to tell if Rochelle is actually serious when she punishes the children or if the narrator exaggerated it for comedy.noteIn one episode, Rock claims that although shethreatenssaid punishments, the only times she follows through is when she tells one of the children “We need to talk”, but this rarely ever happens.. One instance of how far she goes is when she becomes so enraged that she accidentally gets her pump shoved and stuck in Chris' rectum and had him sent to the hospital for trying to be a "bad boy", and (probably because they're all black and no one in the restaurant really cared)got off scot-free with no sort of punishment for nearly killing her oldest child. That said, she isn't outrightcrueland even in her more exaggerated moments such as the above, it's made clear she still cares for her children.In one episode, she tells Julius she knows she’d get arrested if she’d actually went through with her hundreds of threats. In one episode, she tells Julius she knows she’d get arrested if she’d actually went through with her hundreds of threats. Anger Born of Worry: Often after discovering when her family members are safe after dangerous situations, Rochelle will immediately go on a tangent and yell at them for putting themselves in danger. Anti-Hero: She can be quite harsh at times, but on most occasions, she’ll come around by the end.

characters.name: Drew
characters.description: Chris' younger brother, and the middle child of the family. Compared to Chris, he's a lot more popular and talented, being amazing at pretty much everything he sets his mind to just short ofsinging. The Ace: Excels at basically everything, much to Chris's jealousy.When it comes to singing, however, Drew's voice sounds like nails on a chalkboard, to say the least. Always Someone Better: How he is seen by Chris. This is a plot point in "Everybody Hates Drew", where Chris finally admits to Drew that he's tired of Drew being better than him at everything. It's revealed that there's someMutual Envy, as Drew is jealous that their parents always put Chris in charge when they leave the house, and also that Chris can go to school in Brooklyn Beach. Beware the Nice Ones: In one episode, he was willing to forgive Chris for "accidentally" breaking his arm. But when Chris reveals that he did it on purpose,well... Big Brother Bully: In the Season 2 finale, Drew teases Tonya and eventually gets ice cream on his gown and they get a whooping from Rochelle for their misbehaviour.

characters.name: Tonya
characters.description: Chris' younger sister, and the youngest child of the family. She's a very bratty little girl, not helped byher being her father's favorite. Annoying Younger Sibling: In spades. Blackmail: She does this to her brothers frequently. However sometimes, this ends up a favour for them. Bratty Half-Pint: Before becoming aBratty Teenage Daughterin the later seasons. Catchphrase: "I'm telling!"

characters.name: Greg Wuliger
characters.description: Greg is Chris' best friend at school. And probably one of the only real friends he has. Not that he won't run for his own life if Chris is in danger most of the time. Butt-Monkey: Not as much as Chris, but Caruso still picks on him. Catchphrase: “Dude, you are so in there!” Cowardly Lion: Despite the fact that Greg is indeed an"I'm running like a bitch" guy, he had enough nerve to enter the bad part of Bed-Stuy for an untold reason and he manages to dissuade Chris from going along with being a lookout for the "cool kids", a group of delinquents who hang around the Arcade, because Chris would be named an accessory if the cops made the connection between Chris and the "cool kids". After Chris considered his options, he decided to forego being cool andthey both make a run for it after the police show up. Fair-Weather Friend: He has an all-too frequent tendency to run and abandon Chris when things go wrong, usually for Chris himself.

characters.name: Joey Caruso
characters.description: Caruso is the school bully, who frequently harasses Chris, Greg, and other kids. Affably Evil: Not evil, but in the series finale, he waits to punch Greg and changes his treatment of Chris. The Bully: He tends to call Chris names and beat him up every now and then. Characterization Marches On: In "Everybody Hates the Pilot", Caruso is played more as a bully whose racism is even more evident by calling Chris the N-Word. In episodes following that, though Caruso is still a racist, he doesn't ever drop the N-bomb again (though he does come close to calling him "a silly Negro"), preferring to be more creative with his put-downs towards Chris. Even Bullies Have Standards: He gives Chris a break after learning his grandpa died and doesn't enjoy Chris cursing for comedy.

characters.name: Ms. Vivian Morello
characters.description: Chris' teacher, and later the principal of the school. She's always saying something that offends Chris. Bigot with a Crush: She always makes innocently racist comments towards Chris (even calling his race "you people" on a regular basis), but she also has an attraction for black men. Condescending Compassion: Displays this to Chris as often as she can. For example, telling him that she understands he grew up without a father (believing the stereotype that black fathers walk out on their kids). When Chris responds that hedoeshave a father, she responds "if only your mother knew his name!". Flat Character: Sort of. She never really displays any character other than her paradoxical 'racism towards black men' and 'attraction towards black men' traits. Innocent Bigot: She keeps unwittingly making patronizing comments to Chris that sound racist. In one episode she tries to help him get into a school, but tells the interviewer she believes his mother is a drug addict and Chris is a crack baby, based onzero evidenceapart from the fact that Chris is black and tends to misbehave a little sometimes. In another episode, Greg is staying at Chris' house for a while, and when she learns of this she tells him it's nothing to be ashamed about as "Chris' people have a history of being up when the rooster crows to go to work in the fields. Who could expect you to stay up? Go back to sleep.".

characters.name: Lisa
characters.description: A fellow Corleone student. She is almost always antagonizing Chris. Academic Alpha Bitch: She's an exemplary student and is constantly trying to better Chris or otherwise boss him around. Asian and Nerdy: She takes her studies very seriously. Insufferable Genius: She's clearly very smart, but also unnecessarily rude and confrontational. Intrepid Reporter: In "Everybody Hates Promises", where she's constantly trying to get Chris impeached so she can get a juicy scoop.

characters.name: Mrs. Abigail Milone
characters.description: The school monitor who was later promoted to Assistant Principal. Child Hater: As Narrator!Chris puts it, one thing she hates more than children is working with them. Jerkass: She is said to dislike children and can sometimes unnecessarily cruel towards Chris. Reasonable Authority Figure: In her later appearances, she can be this. What Happened to the Mouse?: Disappears after Season 2.

characters.name: Keisha Ridenhour
characters.description: Chris' neighbor of whom he has a crush on. She just sees him as a friend and prefers Drew, though. All Love Is Unrequited: Chris likes her, but she has eyes for his brother Drew. Kick the Dog: Yelling at Chris at the Halloween party when he asks for a dance. To be fair, she did invite Drew, who gave Chris the invite so he could go trick-or-treating,but she didn't need to be so cruel. Literally Loving Thy Neighbor: She lives right next door to Chris and he has a huge crush on her. Put on a Bus to Hell: She leaves Bed-Stuy in the Season 2 premiere. Where to?Compton. We get aCutaway Gagofher family's car getting shot up.

characters.name: Tasha Clarkson
characters.description: A girl who moves next to Chris' family in Season 2. Catchphrase: "Boy, you so crazy!" Clingy Jealous Girl: Once they start officially dating, she becomes far too clingy and needy for even Chrisand they break up. Love Interest: To Chris for a while. Ship Tease: Consistently with Chris, in contrast to Keisha. They even kiss and temporarily get together.

characters.name: Cleavon "James" Barris
characters.description: A boy who lives in the neighborhood and goes to school with Tonya. Always Someone Better: In "Everybody Hates Minimum Wage", he always gets hired at the last place Chris quits due to low pay/taxation and ends up being paid fairly. Everyone Calls Him "Barkeep": Everyone refers to him as James. His real name is only revealed in Season 4. Free-Range Children: He at least has a mother at home, but she never makes an appearance while he's always hanging around with grown-ups and hoodlums. I Have This Friend: This seems to be a common tactic of his. When trick-or-treating, he tries to scam Julius out of more candy by claiming that the previous two visits were his "brother". Then, when in the Big Brother program with Chris, he refers to a "Cleavon" as if he were a different person, when Cleavon is actually his real name.

characters.name: Risky
characters.description: A black market dealer who sells stolen or illegal items. A Day in the Limelight: Works Julius' paper route with him in "Everybody Hates Math", only to try and sell his stolen goods out of the back of the truck. Failed a Spot Check:Played for Laughswhere he tries selling Julius his own work-suit back to him, complete with his name tag on it. Lovable Rogue: Sells stolen goods and is generally shady but also good natured and amicable. Phrase Catcher: "Damn Risky!"

characters.name: Doc Harris
characters.description: A convenience store owner whom Chris works for. Cool Old Guy: He's a jolly fellow who is one of the few people consistently nice to Chris. Notably, he came over to pay his respects when Chris' grandfather passes, installs a new game into his store to cheer him up, and makes some macaroni and cheese for Thanksgiving when Chris accidentally burns it. Dirty Old Man: His girlfriend inEverybody Hates Doc'sis an extremely attractive woman at least a decade younger than him.Subvertedin that he doesn't have evil intent to her. The Hero: In the episode Everybody Hates Bed-Stuy, he inspires Chris to stop lying and make things right. Intergenerational Friendship: He's one of the few consistently friendly people to Chris.

characters.name: Jimmy "Monk" Harris
characters.description: Doc's nephew and co-manager, who is also an ex-soldier. Actor Allusion: "Whatchu talkin' 'bout, Monk?" Disappeared Dad: His father abandoned him when he asked Monk for some money for a bus ticket. Hair-Trigger Temper: A foolproof way to set him off is to remind him of his time in the army,which is extremely easy to do. Only Known by Their Nickname: The name "Monk" is just a nickname, most likely his callsign from back in 'Nam.

characters.name: Louise Clarkson
characters.description: Chris's rude, uptight neighbor and grandmother of his crush Tasha. Deadpan Snarker: Has a very cutting tongue. Even Evil Has Loved Ones: Loves her granddaughter Tasha. Hate Sink: Rude, nosy, condescending, generally not a nice lady in the slightest. Mama Bear: Is VERY protective of Tasha, especially where Chris is concerned. She tells Rochelle to keep Chris from Tasha when rumours of Tasha and Chris spread across Bed-Stuy due to Chris.

characters.name: Jerome
characters.description: Another friend of Chris. He gives Chris advice in exchange for a dollar. Catchphrase: "Hey! Little dude from across the street! Let me hold a dollar." Cool Big Bro: He has this dynamic with Chris after he warms up to him. For all of his hustling, he does genuinely care for Chris. Disappeared Dad: Implied that he is a deadbeat dad. He once offered Chris some tips on how to save money in child support. Easily Forgiven: Chris befriends him rather quickly.

characters.name: Mr. Omar
characters.description: A sleazy funeral director who lives in the same apartment building as the Rock family, renting out an adjacent suite as their tenant. Ascended Extra: He only appeared in a single scene in Season One before becoming the Rocks' tenant for the rest of the series. Catchphrase: "TRAGIC!" Characterization Marches On: He was bald in his first appearance, and he lacked most of the attributes that would define him in his later appearances. Everyone Has Standards: In 'Everybody Hates Snow Day', he is asked by Julius to watch Drew and Tonya. While reluctant at first, he ends up having fun playing Monopoly with them and sticks to his promise even after his widow of the week angrily decides to leave.

characters.name: Malvo
characters.description: A notorious criminal with whom Chris has a number of run-ins. Being Evil Sucks: Ultimately decides to change his criminal ways and pursue an education. Beta Couple: He and Peaches, Tasha's mother, become this to Rochelle and Julius. Big Bad: Of his first two appearances. Intergenerational Friendship: With Chris,after he reforms, though Rochelle and Julius are still wary of him.

characters.name: Edgar "Kill Moves" Devereaux
characters.description: A local homeless man. Not very sane. His nickname comes from the fact that he knows all kinds of deadly martial arts. Cloudcuckoolander: As mentioned, he doesn't have a tight grasp on reality. Crazy Homeless People: Homeless and almost completely insane. He does sharpen up a bit when visiting his motherbut goes right back to being a crazy old hobo once he's back in Bed-Stuy. Everyone Calls Him "Barkeep": He's only referred to as his real name by his mother, naturally. Hidden Depths: Turns out he's a skilled pilot, and that was his occupation before the layoffs under President Reagan.
